tax: Placed at genus rank, Metalcoil is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a serpentine native-copper body. A new species belongs here only if it shows a coiling conductive copper body.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a coiling metallic serpent. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — verdigris smear and arc-marks — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Metalcoil occupies copper-seamed wet galleries. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where earth-charge animates a copper seam and recur with the seam rebuilds the ribbon, sustaining themselves on earth-charge through copper.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: rears and discharges through the wet floor. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: ductile copper body and field-sense. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: electrical discharge and copper constriction. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Metalcoil matters because lethal to wet-shod copper crews. It lets survey reports sort coiling conductive forms from massive ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Metalcoil is the genus-rank grouping of serpentine conductive-copper elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a coiling native-copper ribbon that discharges and constricts, and divide below into species groups by ribbon length and seam charge. A copper serpent that strikes with current.
